advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test case where the replacement lock has a DIFFERENT inode but a back-dated (stale) mtime, so the device+inode identity guard is exercised in isolation from the age re-check.
evidence: test injects `lock_file.write_text(fresh_marker)  # new inode, fresh mtime` at tests/interfaces/store/test_stale_lock_reclaim_write.py:91
gitutil.py:124 `if time.time() - st2.st_mtime <= _INDEX_LOCK_STALE_S: return` would already abort on the fresh mtime before the inode guard is ever the deciding factor
gitutil.py:122 `if (st2.st_dev, st2.st_ino) != (st.st_dev, st.st_ino): return` — the identity guard whose independent behavior is not exercised
advisory.finding: The inode-replacement regression test does not isolate the device+inode identity guard: the injected peer file has a fresh (current) mtime, so the age re-check at line 124 alone would abort the unlink. A bug in the st_dev/st_ino comparison (line 122) would be masked by the age guard, leaving that guard untested.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Drop or replace the `st_ino != stale_ino` assertion, which can spuriously fail on inode-number reuse after unlink+recreate; the content assertion already proves the peer file survived.
evidence: `assert lock_file.stat().st_ino != stale_ino, "should be the fresh replacement inode"` at line 100
peer does `lock_file.unlink()` then `lock_file.write_text(fresh_marker)` (lines 90-91) — the freed inode can be reallocated to the new file by the OS
advisory.finding: The final assertion `lock_file.stat().st_ino != stale_ino` may be flaky: after unlink()+write_text() the filesystem is free to reuse the just-freed inode number for the new file, so st_ino can equal stale_ino even though the swap occurred.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deterministic test where `_reclaim_probe` refreshes the lock's mtime in place (same inode, current time) so the reclaim aborts via the mtime re-check at gitutil.py:124 and the lock survives.
evidence: src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py:124: `if time.time() - st2.st_mtime <= _INDEX_LOCK_STALE_S:` — a distinct abort branch (same inode, refreshed mtime)

tests/interfaces/store/test_stale_lock_reclaim_write.py:89-91: `_peer_replaces_lock` unlinks then re-writes → produces a NEW inode, exercising only the `(st2.st_dev, st2.st_ino) != ...` branch (line 122), not the mtime branch (line 124)
ABSENCE: search for `st_mtime` under tests/interfaces/store returned no matches — no test bumps mtime in place while preserving inode
src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py:118-121: `try: st2 = os.stat(lock_file) except OSError: return  # already gone (a peer reclaimed it first)`
tests/interfaces/store/test_stale_lock_reclaim_write.py:89-91: the only probe re-creates the file, so the post-probe path always exists — the OSError branch is never hit
ABSENCE: no test leaves the path empty after the probe
advisory.finding: The added mtime re-validation branch (`if time.time() - st2.st_mtime <= _INDEX_LOCK_STALE_S: return` — a lock refreshed in place at the SAME inode, so device+inode match but it is now live) is not exercised by any test. Both new tests only cover the inode-CHANGE abort and the stationary-reclaim base case; a refactor that dropped the mtime re-check while keeping the inode check would still pass all tests yet reintroduce the TOCTOU bug for an in-place-refreshed lock.
merged_from: The abort branch where the peer has already removed the stale lock (leaving the path empty, so the pre-unlink `os.stat(lock_file)` raises OSError and the function returns) is not covered by any test.
scenarios: A peer holds the lock and refreshes it in place (rewriting/truncating the same inode) between rebar's staleness decision and its unlink; the mtime-only re-check is the sole guard, and it is untested.
advisory.suggested_fix: Add a test analogous to the inode-replacement one whose probe does `os.utime(lock_file, None)` (or rewrites contents without changing the inode) to bump mtime to now, then assert `lock_file.exists()` and that the inode is unchanged (`== stale_ino`).

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the device+inode identity guard in isolation.
coaching.subject: the device+inode identity guard in isolation

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the in-place mtime-refresh reclaim branch.
coaching.subject: the in-place mtime-refresh reclaim branch

coaching.coaching: File a follow-on ticket (discovered_from this change) for inode-reuse flakiness in the replacement assertion, a pre-existing / out-of-scope issue — do not fix it in this change.
coaching.subject: inode-reuse flakiness in the replacement assertion
